title: Neltuma piurensis
intro: Neltuma piurensis, also known as Piure or Cat's Claw Vine, is a climbing vine native to the tropical regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating digestive issues, immune system disorders, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Neltuma piurensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neltuma piurensis include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of chronic diseases.
taxonomy: Neltuma piurensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Peru. The classification of Neltuma piurensis is still being researched and documented, but it is generally considered to be a member of the genus Neltuma. The Annonaceae family, to which Neltuma piurensis belongs, consists of over 2,000 species of flowering plants. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of Neltuma piurensis within the plant kingdom.
